Off Calendar Nov-03-2009 Continued To Jan-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-03-2009 Continued To Jan-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Small for Lee R. Richards. Robert Elliott for Charles E. Handy. Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator). Charles Trea for Diana Jones.

Mr. Triay stated that Debra Dolch has been appointed the Special Administrator, that they are in discovery, and that they may mediate. The Court inquired whether they could mediate before discovery. Mr. Elliott stated that Mr. Triay stated he wished to obtain discovery before mediation. Mr. Triay stated that he hasn't had much luck with early mediation and noted that they are close to selecting a mediator. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 5, 2010. A status conference is set for January 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for Diana Gail Jones (Petitioner). Steven samll for Lee Richards. Ann Marie Paolini for Charles Handy.

Mr. Epstein stated that his Client filed a contest to both petitions and that all Parties agreed to continue the matters to November 3, 2009 to be heard with Ms. Jones' petition. The matter was continued to November 3, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-24-2009 Continued To Sep-15-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Small for Lee Richards. Anne Paolini for Charles Handy.

Mr. Small stated that he filed his petition with another will. Ms. Paolini stated that both wills were lost and that the way to proceed was to file a petition for special administration. The matter was continued to September 15, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
